titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 589

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=931 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:47:55.701845+00 | translation moa=930 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:47:55.690284+00

p.cl: . W . dei gratia Rex Scott’ . Episcopis . Abbatibus . Comitibus . Baronibus . Vicecomitibus . Prepositis . Ministris . et Omnibus probis Hominibus totius terre sue Clericis et Laicis Salutem .

Sciant presentes
et futuri me concessisse . et hac carta mea confirmasse conuentionem factam inter Ecclesiam sancti Andree . et Ecclesiam sancti Cudberti de Dunelmo . et inter Rogerum Electum Sancti
Andree . et Bertramum priorem . et conuentum ecclesie Sancti Cudberti de Dunelmo . Scilicet quod prefatus Electus concessit deo et sancto Cudberto et prenominato priori et conuentui Dunelmensis ecclesie quod
Ecclesia de coldingham cum capellis ad eam pertinentibus libera sit et quieta sit in perpetuum a Cano et Coneuetho . et procurationibus et hospiciis . et Omnibus aliis exactionibus et
sinodalibus et auxiliis . et Omnibus aliis tam ab ipso Electo quam a successoribus suis . et ab Omnibus officialibus Episcopatus Sancti Andree Salua tamen ipsi Electo et successoribus suis
canonica iusticia et odebientia . Et quod omnes alie parrochialies ecclesie quas prefatus conuentus Dunelmensis ecclesie in Episcopatu Sancti Andree possidet  libere sint et quiete in perpe
tuum a cano et Coneuetho . et procurationibus et hospiciis tam a predicto Electo quam a successoribus suis nisi gratis  huiusmodi facere uoluerint . Nec aliquid in predictis ec
clesiis parrochialibus magis exigetur ab officialibus episcopatus Sancti Andree . quam in Ecclesiis parrochialibus Abbatis Kalcoensis uel alicuius Abbatis qui liberius et Melius tenet in Epi
scopatu sancti Andree Et Quando comune auxilium ponetur per totum Episcopatum sancti Andree  prior et predictus conuentus ecclesie Dunelmensis . de prescriptis ecclesiis suis parrochialibus pre
dicto Electo et successoribus suis respondebunt  Sicut Abbates uel Priores qui liberius tenent in Episcoptu sancti Andree  faciunt de suis . Prior itaque et antedictus conuentus
Dunelmensis ecclesie habebunt in perpetuum tantum potestatem . dignitatem et dispositionem in omnibus ecclesiis suis parrochialibus  quantumcumque alique abbacie habent in
ecclesiis suis que liberius et melius tenent in Episcopatu sancti Andree . Et propter predictas quietantias et libertates . et pro bono pacis predictus Bertramus prior . et conuentus ecclesie
Dunelmensis de consilio et Assensu Hugonis Dunelmensis Episcopi et totius Capituli sui dederunt et concesserunt Deo et ecclesie sancti Andree . et predicto Electo . et successoribus
eius cappellam de Naithanesthurn’ tenendam et cui uoluerint concedendam in perpetuum liberam et quietam et absolutam tam ab eis quam a matrice ecclesia de
Edenham . Quare uolo ut ista conuentio prescripta  inter prenominatas ecclesias firmiter et inuiolabiliter obseruetur  sicut scriptum autenticum inter predictum Electum sancti
Andree . et prefatum . B . priorem et conuentum Dunelmensis ecclesie factum  testatur .

Testibus Hugone Cancellario meo . Ærkenbaldo Abbate de Dunfermel’ . Willelmo Abbate de sancta cruce
Simone Archidiacono Glasg’ . Willelmo maleuicino Archidiacono Laodonie . Rollando . filio uchtredi Comite Dunecano . Willelmo de lindesia . Robert de Londoniis . Rannulf’
de Sol’ . Willelmo de Ramesh’ . Henrico de graham . Willelmo de Cunninghesburgh’.
William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting.

Let present and future know me to have conceded, and by this my charter to have made firm, the agreement made between the church of St Andrews and the church of St Cuthbert at Durham, and between Roger bishop elect of St Andrews and Prior Bertram and the convent of St Cuthbert at Durham, that is to say, that the afore-mentioned bishop elect has conceded to God and St Cuthbert and the afornamed prior and convent of the church of Durham that the church of Coldingham with the chapels belonging to it be free and be quit for ever from cain and conveth, procurations and hospitality, and all other demands and synodal dues and aids, and all other things, as much from the bishop elect himself as from his successors, and from all officials of the bishopric of St Andrews, save only canonical justice and obedience to the bishop elect and his successors; and that all other parish churches which the aforementioned convent of the church of Durham possesses in the bishopric of St Andrews be free and quit for ever from cain and conveth, procurations and hospitality, as much from the aforesaid bishop elect as from his successors, unless they wish to make it freely. And nothing more in the aforesaid parish churches shall be demanded by the officials of the bishopric of St Andrews than in the parish churches of the abbot of Kelso or of any other abbot who holds the more feely and better in the bishopric of St Andrews. And when common aid is levied throughout the whole bishopric of St Andrews, the prior and aforesaid convent of the church of Durham shall answer for their afore-written parish churches to the aforesaid bishop elect and his successors, just as the abbots or priors who freely hold in the bishopric of St Andrews do in respect of their own. The prior and aforesiad convent of the church of Durham shall therefore have in perpetuity such authority, dignity and freedom of disposition in all their parish churches as any other abbeys have in their own churches which they hold the better and more freely in the bishopric of St Andrews. And on account of the aforesaid quittances and liberties, and for the sake of peace, the said Prior Bertram and convent of the church of Durham, by the advice and agreement of Hugh, bishop of Durham, and his whole chapter, have given and conceded to God and the church of St Andrwes and the aforesaid bishop elect and his successors the chapel of Nenthorn to be held and conceded to whom they wish in perpetuity, free, quit and released as much from them as from the mother church of Ednam. Wherefore I will that this agreement aforewritten between the aforenamed churches be strictly and inviolably observed, just as the authentic document produced between the aforesaid bishop elect of St Andrews and the aforementioned Prior Bertram and convent of the church of Durham bears witness.

As witnesses: Hugh my chancellor, Archibald the abbot of Dunfermline, William the abbot of Holyrood, Simon the archdeacon of Glasgow, William Malveisin the archdeacon of Lothian, Roland son of Uhtred, Earl Duncan, William of Lindsay, Robert of London, Ranulf de Soulis, William of Ramsay, Henry Graham, William of Conisbrough.
